Cripps and lord Lloyd , and be to be sell at their shop in Popes-head alley . 1654. to the reader . reader , a tyrant test , and a protector portraiture , be worthy thy contemplation in these froward time ; in the one thou may read what thy condition have be through god most righteous severity : have he not gracious interpose in the other what thy condition be , and may expect to be through his mere mercy so interpose . tyranny make earth a hell , and a tyrant be a devil incarnate . just government make heaven on earth , and good prince , god in the likeness of man . no government be hell break loose , where all will rule , and none be rule ; every man lust will be every man law ; his want measure by his will , and his desert by his desire , which will render man fury in flesh ; and daily tormentor to themselves and other , and therefore any government be better than none , tyranny than anarchy , but just government banish the wicked from a nation , ( as it do the evil angel out of heaven ) make the remain inhabitant to shout for joy ; here thou shall find a tyrant try , and a protector portraiture , by plain character , brief instance , and example of both , ( which true consider with our own concernment in both respect ) will muzzle the mouth of our mutter murmurour , and render we more sensible of our present happiness , and thankful for it ; thou shall not here find a censorious condemnation of the long and short parliament , nor a flatter congratulation of all public transaction since their date ; for although these be ad nos in respect of their event , yet be they supra nos in respect of our censure , this be my principle , this be my prayer , that wherein man have be wise , and do worthy for their country liberty , and the saint interest , God will remember it , and never forget it ; and that wherein they have be weak , and fail in their duty , be but flesh and blood , and man at best , though the best of man , God will forget it , and never remember it ; the demonstration of tyranny , the commendation of magistracy , the characterize of good and bad magistrate , in their principle and practise , with the effect thereof to themselves , and their people , be the design of my heart , have the fact answer the fancy , and the product the project . reader , if thou be a son of Zion , and a citizen of Jerusalem , which be from above , the peace that be within thy gate , and the prosperity that be within thy palace , must needs render thou sensible of thy felicity by thy freedom from tyranny , and fruition of liberty by our present government , thankful for it , and fruitful under it , except like jessurun thou be wax fat , and kick up the heel , have turn thy grace into wantonness , and thy table be make a snare unto thou ; if thou be one of they that have thus convert their fullness into folly , and their liberty into licentiousness , murmur that Moses and Aaron ( i mean ) thy quondam preserver be thy present protector , and have forget the day of old , and the year of many generation , who have sow that crop in tear , yea in blood , which thou now reap in joy , if that liberty will not satisfy , the like whereof be not in all the world , that liberty the like whereof the generation that be past do never understand ; that liberty , a great than which thou know not how to desire ( except it be to have power to tyrannize it over thy brethren dissent from thou , as precious in the eye of the lord as thy self , and it may be more in the truth than thy self ) not a son of Belial suffer to molest thou , nor a dog to move his tongue against thou , if thou can not in this fullness sit down with content , who will pity thou if thou rise up , and fall ? if thou will read and consider the difference between good and bad magistrate , thou may see the misery which thy forefather feel , and our self but late fear under the one , and thy present felicity , which our forefather desire , and we now enjoy under the other , the due and spiritual effect of which vision upon all our heart , through the grace of our god in Jesus Christ , be the fervent prayer of
